title: Voice Bridge Config
description:
Canonical schema for the configuration object passed to `window.voiceBridge.configure(opts)` (Phase 6). Each consumer ships its own per-repo `js/voice-config.js` (or equivalent module) that calls this with its phonetic dictionary, personas, and chat-target preference. The shape is documented here so consumers stay in sync as the voice bridge evolves.

Reference: `shared/js/voice.js`.

sessionStorageKey.description: localStorage key used to persist the auto-speak session flag. Default: `shared-voice-session`. Each consumer SHOULD prefix this with its own namespace (e.g. `llm-benchmark-voice-session`) so multiple sibling apps in the same browser don't share session state.

personaStorageKey.description: localStorage key used to persist the active persona id. Default: `shared-voice-persona`. Same namespacing recommendation as `sessionStorageKey`.

storagePrefix.description: Prefix consumer-scoped storage keys with this string. Reserved for future use (the bridge currently uses the explicit *StorageKey fields).

lang.description: BCP-47 language tag for STT recognition and TTS playback. If null/unset, the bridge falls back to `navigator.language` and then `en-US`.

chatTarget.description:
Where finalized STT transcripts are routed.
  - `auto`: try the canonical Phase 2/3 orb (`window.ChatOrb.dispatch`), then the legacy facade (`window.chatOrb.send`), then `window.chatLLM.handle`. Recommended for new consumers.
  - `chat-orb`: only try the orb path. Use when the consumer has fully migrated off the legacy chat-llm router.
  - `chat-llm`: only try chat-llm. Use only as a temporary bridge.

phoneticReplacements.description: Pronunciation overrides. Keys are matched case-insensitively as whole words; longer keys win (so `MI300X` beats `MI300` if both are present). The replacement string is what TTS actually speaks. Use this to fix domain-specific pronunciations (e.g. acronyms, model names). Multiple `configure({})` calls MERGE into the existing dictionary.

personas.description: Map of persona id → persona config. Persona-aware fields (rate, pitch, voice, lang) are applied to every TTS call when that persona is active (see `setPersona(id)`).

defaultPersona.description: Persona id to set active on first load (only if the user has not already pinned a different persona via `/voice persona <id>`).

persona.description: Per-persona voice tuning. All fields are optional; the bridge falls back to browser defaults when a field is missing.

voice.description: Hard-coded SpeechSynthesisVoice name (case-insensitive). Prefer `preferred_voices` for graceful fallback.

preferred_voices.description: Ordered list of preferred SpeechSynthesisVoice names; the first one found on the user's machine wins. Same field name and semantics as `demo-track.schema.json` so `say()` calls coming from the demo engine and the orb behave identically.

tone.description: Free-text descriptor of the persona's intended voice tone. Not consumed by the bridge directly — exposed for downstream consumers (e.g. an executive-narrator skill) that want to surface tone hints in the UI.

audience.description: Per-persona renderer-side preferences (decimal places, dollar signs, etc.). Not consumed by the bridge directly — passed through verbatim so consumers can read `voiceBridge.getPersona().audience` and adjust their own rendering. See `llm-benchmark/data/present-script.json` for a worked example.

openings.description: Persona-specific opening lines (used by the executive-narrator skill in llm-benchmark).

transitions.description: Persona-specific transition phrases (next, drill-in, back, compare, recommendation).
